prompt_text: Best SaaS analytics platforms for track subscription metrics and MRR from Stripe in real time
response:
The strongest SaaS analytics platforms for **real-time subscription metrics and MRR from Stripe** are **Stripe Billing Analytics / Sigma, Baremetrics, ChartMogul, ProfitWell (Paddle/ProfitWell Metrics), and Illumi**, with Databox or general BI tools as good complements depending on your stack.[1][2][3][4][8][9]

Below is a concise comparison focused on: *real-time Stripe integration, MRR accuracy, SaaS metrics depth, and ease of use*.

| Tool | Best for | Stripe MRR in real time | Key SaaS metrics & features | Notes / Tradeoffs |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Stripe Billing Analytics** | Early-stage or simple stacks that want everything *inside Stripe* | **Native & real-time** inside Stripe Dashboard; no sync delays.[1][4] | Built-in charts for **MRR, MRR growth, churned MRR, expansion**, trial conversion, subscription counts, failed payments.[1][2][4] | Fastest to start, zero extra cost beyond Stripe fees. Limited advanced SaaS analytics/segmenting vs dedicated tools. |
| **Stripe Sigma** | SQL-savvy finance/data teams needing full flexibility on live Stripe data | Directly queries **live Stripe data**; no pipeline refreshes.[1][4] | Can calculate any custom revenue metric (MRR, net retention, cohorts, custom segments) via SQL.[1][4] | Very powerful but requires SQL; charged per query/volume. Best as your “analytics engine” if you have someone technical. |
| **Baremetrics** | Founders who want **one-click, opinionated SaaS dashboards** | Direct Stripe integration; metrics **populate automatically in real time** once connected.[1][3][7][9] | Out-of-the-box **MRR, ARR, churn, LTV, ARPU, CAC, cohort views**, plus cancellation insights and dunning (“Recover”).[1][3][7][9] | Very fast to set up and SaaS-focused. Less customizable schema than ChartMogul/Sigma, and paid from ~$100+/month.[1] |
| **ChartMogul** | Teams needing **API-first, multi-source subscription analytics** | Connects to Stripe and other billing systems; continuously syncs subscription data.[1][2][7][9] | **MRR, ARR, churn, LTV, cohorts**, revenue movements, segmentation by plan, region, etc.; strong API and data import/export.[1][2][7][9] | Better when you’ll have multiple billing sources or complex data modelling. Setup a bit more involved than Baremetrics. |
| **ProfitWell Metrics (via Paddle)** | Budget-conscious teams wanting **free core metrics** | Pulls data from Stripe and other processors for **MRR, churn, LTV**, etc.[2][5][9] | Core subscription metrics: **MRR, churn, LTV**, revenue trends.[2][5] | Free tier is attractive; fewer customization and advanced analytics than Baremetrics/ChartMogul. Owned by Paddle. |
| **Illumi** | Product/ops teams wanting **modern SaaS analytics UI** across metrics | Integrates with Stripe to track MRR, churn, LTV, and subscription metrics.[9] | Focus on **MRR, churn, LTV, subscription health**, often with usage/product lenses.[9] | Good balance of UX + SaaS focus; newer vs incumbents, so ecosystem/docs smaller. |
| **Databox / BI dashboards** | Teams wanting **custom dashboards** blending Stripe with other data (CRM, product, marketing) | Has a Stripe MRR template; pulls Stripe data on a schedule for dashboards.[1][8] | Visual dashboards for **MRR, growth, churn**, plus any other KPIs you wire in.[8] | Great for **executive dashboards**, but metric definitions come from your queries; less “SaaS-native” than Baremetrics/ChartMogul. |

### How to choose (based on your situation)

If you want a concrete recommendation, pick according to your constraints:

- **Fastest, lowest-friction way to see real-time MRR from Stripe (no extra tools):**  
  Use **Stripe Billing Analytics** first (Billing → Analytics → MRR reports).[1][2][4]  
  You’ll get **real-time MRR, MRR growth, churn, trial conversion**, and filtering by product/price out of the box.[1][2]

- **Best “plug it in and forget it” SaaS dashboards for Stripe:**
  - **Baremetrics** if you want opinionated dashboards, cancellation insights, and dunning in one place.[1][3][7][9]
  - **ProfitWell Metrics** if budget is tight and yo
